text:
Market today: Headlines point to a tough environment for retail traders, with recent outperformance ending and ripple effects from an Asian market crash. This suggests broader market weakness, which can hit your tech/chip heavy book. A positive note on Chinese AI innovation could be a future tailwind for chips.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are your biggest concerns. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning their deep losses are amplified, and they decay over time. SMH is also down (-10.6%) and flagged for TRIM, adding to the chip sector risk. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and their stops. Also, monitor the broader sentiment for retail traders, as current trends are unfavorable.

Bottom line: Focus on aggressive risk management for your leveraged, losing positions to protect your capital in a challenging retail market.
plays: SOXL: Respect the default stop at 200 or adjust lower. This leveraged ETF is down 44.02% and decays; aggressive risk management is crucial.
SPAL: Consider trimming or setting a tighter stop than the default 21.38. This new leveraged ETF is down 41.51% and moves fast.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The current market sentiment for retail traders and your existing leveraged losses suggest caution over new entries.
